original: Parliamentary elections were held in Colombia on 9 March 2014 to elect members to both chambers of Congress. The nationwide constituency for the 102-member Senate was contested, as well as the 166 seats of the House of Representatives, plus the delegates to the Andean Parliament. There were 773 candidates for the Senate, 1,528 candidates for the House of Representatives, and 23 candidates for the five Colombian seats in the Andean Parliament. 32,795,962 Colombians had been registered to vote in the elections by the cut-off date of 25 January 2014.The elections were notable for the decision by former president Álvaro Uribe to stand for the Senate, the first ex-president in modern Colombian history to run for Congress afterwards. Uribe is constitutionally barred from standing for president again, having already
paraphrase: On March 9, 2014, Colombia conducted parliamentary elections to select representatives for both its legislative bodies, Congress. The electoral process involved competition for the 102 positions in the Senate, which constituted a single national constituency. Additionally, all 166 seats in the House of Representatives were up for election, alongside the delegates who would represent the nation in the Andean Parliament. A total of 773 individuals vied for a Senate seat, while 1,528 candidates sought election to the House of Representatives. Furthermore, 23 candidates competed for the five designated Colombian seats within the Andean Parliament. By the voter registration deadline of January 25, 2014, a substantial 32,795,962 Colombian citizens were officially registered and eligible to cast their votes in these elections. A particularly noteworthy aspect of these elections was the decision of former president Álvaro Uribe to seek a position in the Senate. This marked a significant historical event, as he became the first ex-president in Colombia's contemporary history to pursue election to Congress subsequent to his presidential term. Uribe's ability to run for the presidency again is constitutionally prohibited, given his prior incumbency.

paraphrase: Supriyo, also known as Supriya Chakraborty, and Abhay Dang versus the Union of India, represented by its Secretary, Ministry of Law and Justice, along with other associated legal proceedings (2023), comprises a current series of significant cases before the Supreme Court of India. These cases are scheduled to examine the question of whether the right to marriage and the formation of a family should be granted to LGBTQ+ individuals in India. A Constitution Bench, composed of five judges including Chief Justice of India D.Y. Chandrachud, Justice S.K. Kaul, Justice Ravindra Bhat, Justice Hima Kohli, and Justice P.S. Narasimha, is slated to preside over twenty interconnected cases initiated by a total of fifty-two petitioners. These petitioners, who include same-sex couples and individuals, are seeking legal affirmation of the entitlement to marry and to establish a family. Their arguments are founded upon protections against discrimination, the right to equal treatment, the right to dignity, personal freedom, privacy, self-governance, and the freedom of belief.

paraphrase: The 38th Artios Awards ceremony, which recognizes outstanding originality, creativity, and the significant impact of casting on the overall excellence of various productions including films, television series, short films, short-form series, and theatre, took place on March 9, 2023. This event marked a significant return to in-person gatherings after a two-year hiatus, being simultaneously hosted at two distinct venues: The Beverly Hilton in Los Angeles and the Edison Ballroom in New York. The nominations for the categories spanning television, short-form projects, and theatre were publicly revealed on October 17, 2022. Subsequently, the nominees for the film categories were unveiled on January 10, 2023. The hosting duties for this dual-location ceremony were shared by Yvette Nicole Brown and Amber Ruffin. Notably, Brown had previously served as host for the inaugural virtual awards presentation.

original: The Global Pact for the Environment project was launched in 2017 by a network of experts known as the "International Group of Experts for the Pact" (IGEP). The group is made up of more than a hundred legal experts in environmental law and is chaired by former COP21 President Laurent Fabius.On 10 May 2018, the United Nations General Assembly adopted, by 142 votes in favor, 5 votes against (United States, Russia, Syria, Turkey, and the Philippines) and 7 abstentions (Saudi Arabia, Belarus, Iran, Malaysia, Nicaragua, Nigeria, and Tajikistan), a resolution paving the way for the negotiation of a Global Pact for the Environment (Resolution A/72/L.51 of 10 May 2018, "Towards a Global Pact for the Environment").On 8 May 2020, the United Nations Environment Programme nominated two co-facilitators to
paraphrase: The "International Group of Experts for the Pact" (IGEP), a collective of specialists, initiated the Global Pact for the Environment endeavor in 2017. This assembly comprises over one hundred legal professionals specializing in environmental law, and its leadership is provided by Laurent Fabius, who previously served as the President of COP21. On May 10, 2018, the United Nations General Assembly passed a resolution, designated as Resolution A/72/L.51 and titled "Towards a Global Pact for the Environment," which set the stage for discussions regarding a Global Pact for the Environment. This resolution received 142 affirmative votes, with 5 nations voting against it (the United States, Russia, Syria, Turkey, and the Philippines), and 7 nations abstaining from the vote (Saudi Arabia, Belarus, Iran, Malaysia, Nicaragua, Nigeria, and Tajikistan). Subsequently, on May 8, 2020, the United Nations Environment Programme appointed two individuals to serve as co-facilitators for this initiative.

original: José Salvador Alvarenga (Spanish: [xoˈse salβaˈðoɾ alβaˈɾeŋɡa]; born c. 1975) is a Salvadoran fisherman and author who was found on January 30, 2014, aged 36 or 37, on the Marshall Islands after spending 14 months adrift in a fishing boat in the Pacific Ocean beginning on November 17, 2012. He survived mainly on a diet of raw fish, turtles, small birds, sharks and rainwater. He swam to shore at Tile Islet, a small island that is part of Ebon Atoll, on January 30. Two locals, Emi Libokmeto and Russel Laikidrik, found him naked, clutching a knife and shouting in Spanish. He was treated in a hospital in Majuro before flying to his family home in El Salvador on February 10.Alvarenga's story was heavily reported worldwide despite initial criticism
paraphrase: José Salvador Alvarenga (Spanish: [xoˈse salβaˈðoɾ alβaˈɾeŋɡa]), whose birth year is around 1975, is a Salvadoran fisherman and writer. He was discovered on January 30, 2014, at the age of 36 or 37, in the Marshall Islands. This discovery occurred after he had spent 14 months cast away in a fishing vessel in the Pacific Ocean, an ordeal that commenced on November 17, 2012. His sustenance during this period primarily consisted of raw fish, turtles, small birds, sharks, and collected rainwater. On January 30, he reached the shoreline of Tile Islet, a minor island belonging to Ebon Atoll, by swimming. Emi Libokmeto and Russel Laikidrik, two local inhabitants, encountered him in a naked state, grasping a knife, and yelling in Spanish. He received medical care at a hospital in Majuro prior to his flight to his family residence in El Salvador on February 10. Alvarenga's narrative garnered extensive global media coverage, notwithstanding initial skepticism.

A Government of the German-speaking Community was formed only a few days after the elections. paraphrase: After the federal and regional elections, which took place concurrently on May 25, 2014, discussions commenced regarding the establishment of a new Federal Government, in addition to new regional administrations for Flanders, Wallonia, the French Community, and Brussels. A Government for the German-speaking Community was constituted merely days subsequent to the electoral process. The preceding federal administration, known as the Di Rupo Government, comprised a three-party alliance representing the three established "ideological families": Christian democrats from both Flemish and French-speaking communities (CD&V/cdH), liberals (Open Vld/MR), and socialists (sp.a/PS). In contrast, the Michel Government, which emerged from these negotiations, is composed of a center-right alliance featuring Flemish nationalists (N-VA), Flemish Christian democrats (CD&V), and the liberal factions (Open Vld/MR); notably, this includes only a single party from the French-speaking community. The newly formed Flemish Government mirrors this exact coalition, while the Governments of Wallonia and the French Community are still being formed.

paraphrase: The winter season of 2014–15 in North America was characterized by intensely cold and exceptionally snowy conditions, particularly throughout the eastern portion of the continent during the months of January, February, and March. The cold weather commenced sooner than usual, with numerous locations across North America encountering their initial winter weather events as early as mid-November. paraphrase: The Gaza Strip is currently experiencing a land, air, and sea blockade, which has been continuously enforced by both Israel and Egypt. This blockade was initially implemented on a temporary basis between 2005 and 2006, becoming a permanent measure starting in 2007. Its establishment followed Israel's withdrawal from Gaza. The decision to make the blockade permanent occurred after Hamas gained control of the Gaza Strip during the conflict known as the Battle of Gaza. During this event, Hamas seized control of governmental bodies and replaced officials affiliated with Fatah and other branches of the Palestinian Authority (PA) with its own members. Prior to this, in March 2006, after Hamas had emerged victorious in elections and subsequently established the PA government under the leadership of Ismail Haniya, Israel, along with the Quartet on the Middle East, stipulated certain conditions. These conditions had to be met before they would continue offering assistance to the PA or engage in any discussions with individuals who were part of a Hamas-led PA government. The specified conditions included acknowledging the state of Israel and renouncing any acts of violence.

original: Eleanor Poppy Miranda de Freitas (26 June 1990 – 4 April 2014) was an English woman who committed suicide three days before the commencement of her trial for perverting the course of justice for allegedly making a false accusation of rape. Her death prompted a debate over whether prosecuting people accused of making a false accusation of rape could deter rape victims from reporting the crime, as well as whether it was appropriate to prosecute vulnerable individuals (de Freitas had been diagnosed with bipolar disorder and depression and was judged by a psychiatrist to represent a suicide risk). The decision to prosecute de Freitas was ultimately upheld by both the Director of Public Prosecutions and the Attorney General for England and Wales, while the Metropolitan Police Service paid
paraphrase: Eleanor Poppy Miranda de Freitas, an English woman born on June 26, 1990, and deceased on April 4, 2014, ended her own life merely three days prior to the scheduled start of her legal proceedings. She was facing charges of obstructing justice, specifically for allegedly fabricating a rape accusation. Her passing ignited a significant public discussion concerning several critical issues: whether pursuing legal action against individuals accused of filing false rape reports might discourage actual victims from coming forward, and whether it is morally justifiable to prosecute individuals who are particularly susceptible, given that de Freitas had been diagnosed with bipolar disorder and depression and a psychiatrist had assessed her as being at risk of suicide. Despite these concerns, the decision to pursue legal action against de Freitas received endorsement from both the Director of Public Prosecutions and the Attorney General for England and Wales, and the Metropolitan Police Service subsequently compensated her family.

original: The war on terror, officially the Global War on Terrorism (GWOT), is a global counterterrorism military campaign initiated by the United States following the September 11 attacks and is also the most recent global conflict spanning multiple wars. The main targets of the campaign were militant Islamist and Salafi jihadist armed organisations such as Al-Qaeda, the Islamic State, and their international affiliates, which were waging military insurgencies to overthrow governments of various Muslim-majority countries. Other major targets included the Ba'athist regime in Iraq, which was toppled during an invasion in 2003, and various militant factions that fought during the ensuing Iraqi insurgency. The "war on terror" was officially declared over in May 2010 and again in May 2013. Other American military campaigns during the 2010s have also been
paraphrase: The United States launched a worldwide military effort against terrorism, formally known as the Global War on Terrorism (GWOT), subsequent to the September 11 attacks. This initiative represents the newest global conflict, encompassing numerous distinct wars. The primary adversaries in this campaign were radical Islamist and Salafi jihadist armed groups, including prominent organizations such as Al-Qaeda and the Islamic State, along with their international branches. These groups were actively engaged in military insurgencies, aiming to depose the governments of several nations predominantly inhabited by Muslims. Additionally, significant objectives included the Ba'athist government in Iraq, which was overthrown during an invasion in 2003, and various armed factions that participated in the subsequent Iraqi insurgency. The "war on terror" was formally concluded on two separate occasions, first in May 2010 and then again in May 2013. Other military operations undertaken by the United States during the 2010s have also been part of this broader counterterrorism effort.

original: In journalism, local news refers to coverage of events, by the news, in a local context that would not be an interest of another locality, or otherwise be of national or international scope. Local news, in contrast to national or international news, caters to the news of their regional and local communities; they focus on more localized issues and events. Some key features of local newsrooms includes regional politics, weather, business, and human interest stories. Local news readership has been declining in recent years, according to a recent study. paraphrase: In the field of journalism, the term "local news" pertains to the reporting of occurrences within a specific local setting, which typically would not capture the attention of communities outside that particular area, nor would it possess a national or international reach. Unlike news that covers national or global topics, local news specifically serves the informational needs of its immediate regional and local populations. Its primary focus is on matters and happenings that are more confined to a particular area. Prominent characteristics of local news organizations often encompass reporting on regional political developments, meteorological updates, commercial activities, and narratives centered on human interest. A recent study has indicated a downward trend in the number of individuals who consume local news through reading. Concurrently, as a growing number of television viewers opt for streaming services, the audience for local news broadcasts is also starting to diminish. Nikki Usher, who holds the position of associate professor at the University of Illinois's College of Media, put forth an argument in her work titled "The Complicated Future of" regarding this phenomenon.

original: Presidential elections were held in Egypt between 26 and 28 May 2014. There were only two candidates, former Egyptian defence minister Abdel Fattah el-Sisi and Egyptian Popular Current candidate Hamdeen Sabahi. El-Sisi won the election in a landslide victory, having received 97% of votes. Before announcing his candidacy in the election, el-Sisi, who as Defence Minister also served as Commander-in-Chief of the Egyptian Armed Forces, was responsible for officially announcing the removal of president Mohamed Morsi from office in the aftermath of the June 2013 Egyptian protests. After Morsi's removal, Sisi installed a temporary interim government, but remained Egypt's Minister of Defence and assumed the role of the country's First Deputy Prime Minister. On 26 March 2014, he officially retired from the military, and announced that he would
paraphrase: Between May 26 and May 28, 2014, Egypt conducted its presidential elections. The ballot featured only two contenders: Abdel Fattah el-Sisi, who previously served as Egypt's defense minister, and Hamdeen Sabahi, representing the Egyptian Popular Current. El-Sisi secured a decisive win in the election, garnering an overwhelming 97% of the total votes cast. Prior to declaring his intention to run for president, el-Sisi, in his capacity as both Defense Minister and Commander-in-Chief of the Egyptian Armed Forces, had publicly declared the removal of President Mohamed Morsi from power. This declaration followed the widespread Egyptian protests that occurred in June 2013. Subsequent to Morsi's ousting, Sisi established a provisional interim government. He concurrently retained his position as Egypt's Minister of Defense and took on the duties of the nation's First Deputy Prime Minister. On March 26, 2014, he formally ended his military career and subsequently revealed his intention to pursue the presidency.

original: The Reddings was an American funk, soul and disco band, founded by Otis Redding's sons Dexter (bass and vocals) and Otis Redding III (December 17, 1963 – April 18, 2023 age 59; guitar) together with Mark Lockett (drums, keyboards, and lead vocal).Their most well-known songs include "Remote Control" (1980) / "Doin' it" / "Funkin on the One" / "Class (Is What You Got)" (1981) / "Sittin' on the Dock of the Bay" (1982) / "Hand Dance" (1983) and "The Awakening" (1980), a bass-drums-duet which is often performed live by Les Claypool (Primus) and on his album Highball with the Devil. Their album The Reddings was released on November 26, 1988, and reached a peak position of #88 on the US Billboard album chart. It featured a hit single
paraphrase: An American musical group, The Reddings, specialized in the genres of funk, soul, and disco. It was established by the offspring of Otis Redding: Dexter, who contributed on bass and vocals, and Otis Redding III, who was born on December 17, 1963, and passed away on April 18, 2023, at the age of 59, handling the guitar. Mark Lockett also joined the band, playing drums, keyboards, and serving as the lead vocalist. Among their most recognized musical pieces are "Remote Control" from 1980, "Doin' it," "Funkin on the One," "Class (Is What You Got)" from 1981, "Sittin' on the Dock of the Bay" from 1982, "Hand Dance" from 1983, and "The Awakening" from 1980. The latter is a bass-drums duet that Les Claypool of Primus frequently performs during live shows and is also featured on his album, "Highball with the Devil." The Reddings' album, also titled "The Reddings," was made available to the public on November 26, 1988. This album achieved a notable position on the US Billboard album chart, peaking at number 88. It also contained a successful single.

original: The Anti-Homosexuality Bill, 2023 is a Ugandan parliamentary bill that restricts freedom of speech on LGBT topics and criminalises homosexuality. On 21 March 2023, the bill was read a third time, and was then sent to President Yoweri Museveni for assent. Museveni returned the bill to parliament for reconsideration on 21 April 2023.Under the bill, persons convicted of gay sex are liable imprisonment for life. The bill additionally prescribes the death penalty for 'aggravated homosexuality'. The latter offence includes same-sex rape, sex in a position of authority or procured by intimidation, sex with persons older than seventy-five, and sex with the disabled and mentally ill. Further, under its provisions, promotion (including normalisation) of homosexuality is punishable by imprisonment and fines. The bill has been condemned by the United
paraphrase: The Ugandan legislative proposal known as the Anti-Homosexuality Bill, 2023, aims to curtail freedom of expression concerning LGBT issues and to outlaw homosexual acts. This bill underwent its third reading on March 21, 2023, following which it was transmitted to President Yoweri Museveni for his approval. However, President Museveni referred the bill back to the parliament for further review on April 21, 2023. According to the stipulations of this bill, individuals found guilty of engaging in gay sex face the possibility of life imprisonment. Moreover, the bill institutes the death penalty for what it terms 'aggravated homosexuality.' This more severe offense encompasses various acts such as same-sex rape, sexual acts committed by someone in a position of authority or obtained through coercion, sexual relations with individuals over the age of seventy-five, and sexual encounters involving disabled or mentally ill persons. Additionally, the bill's clauses state that the promotion of homosexuality, which includes its normalization, can lead to penalties involving both imprisonment and monetary fines. This legislative measure has garnered widespread condemnation from the United States.

paraphrase: A domestic dispute within the Central African Republic (CAR) principally commenced on April 13, 2013, coinciding with the formal assumption of power by President Michel Djotodia's administration. This confrontation pitted the government of the Central African Republic's erstwhile Séléka alliance of insurgent factions, predominantly composed of the Muslim demographic, against the largely Christian anti-balaka alliance. This particular dispute was a component of the protracted Central African Republic Civil War, which began in 2012 and continues to the present day. Global bodies, including the United Nations, had issued cautionary statements regarding the potential for widespread ethnic cleansing. United Nations Security Council Resolution 2122 granted permission for the deployment of the African-led International Support Mission to the Central African Republic (MISCA) to the nation, and for France to spearhead military actions with supplementary forces dispatched to augment its presence in the country. Subsequent to a gathering of the Economic Community of Central African States.

It was the sixth motion of no confidence in Spain since the country's transition to democracy.Vox announced the motion of no confidence on 9 December 2022, proposing it to be tabled under an independent candidate with government experience, voicing that Sánchez's proposed reforms of the Criminal Code to modify the embezzlement and sedition crimes were akin to a "self-coup" similar to "that of Pedro Castillo in Peru" two days earlier. paraphrase: On February 27, 2023, the far-right political party Vox presented a motion of no confidence targeting the Spanish government led by Pedro Sánchez. This motion is scheduled for discussion and a vote in the Congress of Deputies on March 21 and 22, 2023. This marks the sixth instance of a no-confidence motion in Spain since the nation's transition to a democratic system of government. Vox had initially made public its intention to file this motion of no confidence on December 9, 2022. At that time, the party suggested that the motion should be put forward by an independent candidate possessing experience in government. Vox justified this action by asserting that the reforms proposed by Sánchez to the Criminal Code, which aimed to alter the offenses of embezzlement and sedition, amounted to a "self-coup," drawing a parallel to the events involving "Pedro Castillo in Peru" that had transpired two days prior. The People's Party (PP), an opposition party headed by Alberto Núñez Feijóo, declared that its members would not cast votes opposing the motion.

Previously hindered by national caveats, the deteroriating security situation prompted the German-led Regional Command North to launch a series of operations to take on the rising insurgency. Within two years, the German presence would be doubled and additional reinforcements from the American ISAF contingent
paraphrase: This account focuses on the developments in the northern region of Afghanistan that occurred from April 2009 through 2014. Despite this particular area of the nation having generally experienced a greater degree of tranquility compared to the active conflict zones in the southern and eastern parts, hostilities reignited in 2008. During this period, German military personnel stationed in the region faced an increased frequency of assaults, which tragically resulted in the fatalities of multiple soldiers. The worsening security conditions, which had previously been constrained by national restrictions, compelled the German-commanded Regional Command North to initiate a sequence of military actions aimed at confronting the escalating insurgency. Coordinated military efforts were set into motion following an insurgent assault on PRT Kunduz, which took place mere moments after German Chancellor Angela Merkel had concluded her visit and departed. Over the subsequent two years, the German military presence was expanded twofold, and supplementary troops from the American International Security Assistance Force (ISAF) contingent were deployed.

original: The 2022-2023 Pakistan economic crisis is an ongoing economic crisis and part of 2022-2023 political unrest in Pakistan. It has caused severe economic challenges for months due to which food, gas and oil prices have risen. The Russian invasion of Ukraine has caused fuel prices to rise worldwide. Excessive external borrowings by the country over the years raised the spectre of default, causing the currency to fall and making imports more expensive in relative terms. By June 2022, inflation was at an all time high, along with rising food prices.Poor governance and low productivity per capita in comparison with other low to middle-income developing countries have contributed to a balance of payment crisis, where the country is unable to earn enough foreign exchange to fund the imports that
paraphrase: Pakistan has been experiencing an ongoing economic downturn, known as the 2022-2023 Pakistan economic crisis, which is intrinsically linked to the 2022-2023 political instability within the nation. This situation has led to significant economic difficulties over several months, resulting in increased costs for essential goods such as food, gas, and oil. The global increase in fuel prices has been exacerbated by the Russian invasion of Ukraine. A history of substantial external borrowing by the country has created the risk of a financial default, leading to a depreciation of its currency and, consequently, making imported goods more costly when compared to domestic alternatives. By June 2022, the rate of inflation had reached an unprecedented peak, accompanied by a simultaneous surge in food prices. Contributing factors to this balance of payments crisis include substandard governance and a comparatively low level of productivity per person when contrasted with other developing nations in the low to middle-income bracket. This crisis manifests as the country's inability to generate sufficient foreign currency to finance its import needs.

original: 2013–2014 Atlantic winter storms in Europe were a series of winter storms affecting areas of Atlantic Europe and beyond. The French Atlantic coastal regions (chiefly Brittany), South West and Southern England, West Wales, Ireland, Spanish Atlantic coastal regions (particularly Galicia) were especially affected by a "conveyor belt" series of high-precipitation storms (mostly not exceptional for their winds) and by high tides. Many storms were explosively deepened by a strong jet stream, many deepening below 950 hPa. The repeated formation of large deep lows over the Atlantic brought storm surges and large waves which coincided with some of the highest astronomical tides of the year and caused coastal damage. The low pressure areas brought heavy rainfalls which led to flooding, which became most severe over parts of England such
paraphrase: Between 2013 and 2014, a succession of winter weather events known as the Atlantic winter storms impacted various parts of Atlantic Europe and adjacent regions. The coastal areas of the French Atlantic (primarily Brittany), the South West and Southern portions of England, Western Wales, Ireland, and the Spanish Atlantic coastline (especially Galicia) experienced significant effects from a continuous sequence of high-precipitation storms (which were generally not notable for their wind speeds) and elevated tides. A powerful jet stream was responsible for the rapid intensification of numerous storms, many of which deepened to below 950 hectopascals. The recurrent development of substantial deep low-pressure systems over the Atlantic Ocean generated storm surges and considerable waves. These phenomena occurred simultaneously with some of the year's highest astronomical tides, resulting in damage to coastal areas. The low-pressure systems delivered substantial rainfall, which subsequently triggered flooding, with the most severe instances occurring across certain parts of England.

original: General elections were held in Costa Rica on Sunday, 2 February 2014 to elect a new president, two vice presidents, and 57 Legislative Assembly lawmakers. In accordance with Article 132 of the constitution, incumbent President Laura Chinchilla Miranda was ineligible to run for a second consecutive term.The ruling National Liberation Party put forward San José Mayor Johnny Araya Monge as its presidential candidate; the Libertarian Movement party nominated former legislator Otto Guevara Guth; the leftist Broad Front nominated José María Villalta Florez-Estrada; and the center-left Citizens' Action Party nominated Luis Guillermo Solís Rivera. paraphrase: On Sunday, February 2, 2014, general elections took place in Costa Rica. During these elections, citizens voted to choose a new president, two vice presidents, and 57 members of the Legislative Assembly. As stipulated by Article 132 of the constitution, the sitting president, Laura Chinchilla Miranda, was barred from seeking re-election for a subsequent term. The National Liberation Party, which was the governing party, selected Johnny Araya Monge, the Mayor of San José, as their presidential candidate. The Libertarian Movement party put forward former legislator Otto Guevara Guth. José María Villalta Florez-Estrada was the nominee for the leftist Broad Front. Meanwhile, the center-left Citizens' Action Party chose Luis Guillermo Solís Rivera as their candidate. Surveys conducted in December 2013 indicated that Araya was in the lead with 37 percent of the vote, followed closely by Villalta at 32 percent. If previous tournaments lacking hosts are factored in, it would be the twentieth. This biennial international youth football event is orchestrated by the Confederation of African Football (CAF) and features male national teams comprised of athletes who are 17 years old or younger. It was determined in May 2021 that Algeria would serve as the host nation for this particular tournament. This specific edition was slated to be the second instance where the tournament's format had been enlarged to accommodate twelve teams, a change from the previous eight-team structure. The four leading teams from this championship will secure berths in the 2023 FIFA U-17 World Cup, thereby acting as the CAF's designated representatives. Cameroon currently holds the title of reigning champions, having emerged victorious in the 2019 edition of the tournament.

The mosque is located in the centre of Plovdiv and was built in 1363–1364 on the site of the Sveta Petka Tarnovska Cathedral Church after the conquest of Plovdiv by the Ottoman army. paraphrase: The mosque known as Dzhumaya Mosque (Джумая джамия in Bulgarian) can be found in the city of Plovdiv, situated within the Plovdiv Province of Bulgaria. In Turkish, it bears the names Hüdavendigâr Camii or Cuma Camii. This mosque is positioned in the central area of Plovdiv and its construction commenced in the years 1363–1364. It was erected on the very spot where the Sveta Petka Tarnovska Cathedral Church previously stood, following the successful capture of Plovdiv by the Ottoman army. Subsequently, during the 15th century, while Sultan Murad I was in power, the initial structure was torn down and subsequently replaced by the mosque that stands today. It was also referred to as Ulu Dzhumaya Mosque, signifying the Main Friday Mosque. This mosque is quite sizable, featuring nine domes and a spacious prayer hall measuring 33 meters by 27 meters (108 feet by 89 feet). A minaret is conspicuously situated at the northeast corner of its primary façade. The internal wall paintings within the mosque originate from the period spanning the late 18th century to the early 19th century.

The 55th iteration of the Miss Belgium beauty contest, known as Miss Belgium 2023, took place on February 11, 2023. The chosen venue for this event was the Plopsaland Theater, situated in De Panne, Belgium. Due to the inability of the current Miss Belgium, Chayenne Van Aarle, to attend, the title was bestowed upon Emilie Vansteenkiste from Flemish Brabant by Miss Belgium 2021, Kedist Deltour, at the conclusion of the evening's proceedings. Ms. Vansteenkiste is subsequently slated to represent Belgium in the Miss World 2023 competition.

The organizers of the Miss Belgium pageant faced a series of unfortunate incidents commencing in the final week leading up to the event. Chayenne Van Aarle, the reigning Miss Belgium, was involved in a severe car accident just days prior to the scheduled date. Her ongoing recovery necessitated her absence from the event. Furthermore, the start of the event was delayed by several hours owing to concerns regarding a potential terrorist attack. A suspect was apprehended, and weapons were subsequently discovered.

original: MS Norman Atlantic was a roll-on/roll-off passenger (ROPAX) ferry owned by the Italian ferry company Visemar di Navigazione. The ferry was chartered by ANEK Lines from December 2014. On 28 December 2014, she caught fire in the Strait of Otranto, in the Adriatic Sea. At the time of the fire there were an estimated 475 people on board the ship including 417 passengers, 55 crew and at least three illegal immigrants. Of these, 452 people were rescued and the bodies of 11 were recovered. It is known that 16 passengers died and it was estimated that there were 28 deaths overall, the uncertainty arising from estimates of the number of illegal immigrants on board. Additionally, two crewmembers of the Albanian tug Iliria were killed during the salvage operations

Beginning in December 2014, ANEK Lines leased the vessel. A fire broke out on the ship on December 28, 2014, while it was located in the Strait of Otranto, within the Adriatic Sea. During the incident, approximately 475 individuals were present on board, comprising 417 passengers, 55 crew members, and a minimum of three undocumented immigrants. From this total, 452 individuals were successfully rescued, and the remains of 11 people were retrieved. Furthermore, two crew members from the Albanian tugboat Iliria lost their lives while engaged in the salvage operations.

Seven figures—a First Nations fighter, a Métis militiaman, a regular infantryman from the Royal Newfoundland Regiment of Fencible Infantry, a Quebec soldier of the Canadian Voltigeurs being bandaged by a female figure, a Royal Navy marine, and a farmer—represent those who took part on the Canadian side of the War of 1812. paraphrase: The War of 1812 Monument, officially named Triumph Through Diversity (Triomphe grâce à la diversité in French), is a memorial constructed from bronze and stone, positioned on Parliament Hill in Ottawa, Ontario, Canada, which serves as the nation's capital. This monument showcases seven distinct figures: a First Nations combatant, a Métis militiaman, a standard infantry soldier from the Royal Newfoundland Regiment of Fencible Infantry, a Quebecois soldier from the Canadian Voltigeurs receiving medical attention from a female figure, a Royal Navy marine, and a farmer. These figures symbolize the various individuals who participated in the War of 1812 on the Canadian side. The monument is strategically placed opposite the National War Memorial, with one of the figures on the 1812 Monument gesturing towards it. An additional element of the monument is a maple tree, which was planted in soil collected from 10 different Canadian battlefield locations and is regularly watered.

paraphrase: On June 1, 1975, Jacqueline Montgomery, a 15-year-old female resident of Islington, located in North London, United Kingdom, was tragically killed. The perpetrator was Dennis McGrory, who was 28 years old and the estranged partner of Montgomery's aunt. During his court proceedings, McGrory was characterized as being "wild with rage" when he committed the acts of sexual assault, stabbing, and strangulation against Montgomery within her family's residence on Offord Road. Her father made the grim discovery of her body on the living room floor of their house during the early morning hours of June 2. paraphrase: The 76th annual British Academy Film Awards ceremony, often referred to as the BAFTAs, took place on February 19, 2023. This event celebrated the most outstanding domestic and international cinematic achievements of 2022. The venue for the awards was the Royal Festival Hall, situated within the Southbank Centre in London. The British Academy of Film and Television Arts was responsible for presenting these prestigious awards, which recognized the finest full-length films and documentaries, regardless of their country of origin, that had been shown in British cinemas throughout 2022. The list of nominees was unveiled on January 19, 2023, during a worldwide livestream event. Actors Hayley Atwell and Toheeb Jimoh served as hosts for this announcement, which originated from the newly renovated headquarters of the arts charity at 195 Piccadilly, London. The nominees for the EE Rising Star Award, a unique category where the British public casts the votes, were revealed two days earlier, on January 17, 2023. The German-language epic anti-war drama, *All Quiet on the Western Front*, was a significant contender.

original: Action Plan against Disinformation (Czech: Akční plán pro čelení dezinformacím) was a material that was prepared by the government commissioner for media and disinformation Michal Klíma and his team of experts at the government office on behalf of the Cabinet of Petr Fiala. Media learned about the material on December 27, 2022. The strategy proposes possible ways of blocking disinformation, sanctioning its publishers and prosecuting them authors and distributors, but also financial support of "correct media" with tens of millions of crowns per year. The government terminated cooperation with Michal Klíma on February 15, 2023, with immediate effect and abolished the position of government commissioner for media and disinformation, which he had held since the end of March 2022, and transferred the disinformation agenda to the government's national
paraphrase: The document titled Action Plan against Disinformation, known in Czech as Akční plán pro čelení dezinformacím, was developed by government commissioner for media and disinformation Michal Klíma and his dedicated team of specialists within the government office. This initiative was undertaken at the direction of the Cabinet led by Petr Fiala. The public, specifically through media outlets, became aware of this document on December 27, 2022. The outlined strategy put forth various approaches for countering disinformation, such as implementing measures to block its spread, imposing penalties on those who publish it, and pursuing legal action against its creators and disseminators. Additionally, the plan suggested providing substantial financial aid, amounting to tens of millions of crowns annually, to "correct media" organizations. However, on February 15, 2023, the government concluded its collaboration with Michal Klíma, effective immediately. Concurrently, the role of government commissioner for media and disinformation, which Klíma had occupied since late March 2022, was eliminated, and the responsibilities pertaining to disinformation were subsequently transferred to the government's national security advisor.

original: The Suez Canal Corridor Area Project (Arabic: مشروع تطوير محور قناة السويس) is a megaproject in Egypt that was launched on 5 August 2014 by President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi and completed in 2015. The project aimed to increase the role of the Suez Canal region in international trade and to develop the canal cities of Suez, Ismailia, and Port Said. The project involved building a new city (Ismailia), an industrial zone, fish farms, encouraging the development of a tech industry, building seven new tunnels between Sinai and Ismailia and Port Said, improving five existing ports, creating new centers for logistics and ship services, and digging a new canal parallel to the Suez Canal. paraphrase: The Suez Canal Corridor Area Project, known in Arabic as مشروع تطوير محور قناة السويس, represents a significant megaproject situated in Egypt. This undertaking was inaugurated on August 5, 2014, by President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi and reached its conclusion in 2015. The core objective of this initiative was to enhance the Suez Canal region's contribution to global commerce and to foster the growth of the cities along the canal: Suez, Ismailia, and Port Said. The project encompassed several key components: the establishment of a new urban center (Ismailia), the creation of an industrial zone, the development of fish farms, the promotion of a burgeoning technology sector, the construction of seven novel tunnels connecting Sinai with Ismailia and Port Said, the modernization of five pre-existing ports, the establishment of fresh hubs for logistics and maritime services, and the excavation of an additional canal running parallel to the original Suez Canal. This parallel canal was specifically built to augment the canal's capacity, thereby facilitating the simultaneous transit of vessels in opposing directions.

original: The Estación Polar Científica Conjunta Glaciar Unión (or Union Glacier Joint Scientific Polar Station) or Union Glacier Station is a summer Antarctic base of Chile, situated on Union Glacier in the Ellsworth Mountains. It is jointly operated from November to January by the Chilean Antarctic Institute and the three armed forces of Chile. It was inaugurated on 4 January 2014 by Chilean President Sebastián Piñera Echeñique.In 2013, the Antarctic stations Teniente Arturo Parodi Alister and Antonio Huneeus were dismantled and their equipment was transferred to Union Glacier Station. This followed the relocation in November 2010 of the operations of the private American company Antarctic Logistics & Expeditions LLC, which has operated the landing strip on Union Glacier since December 2008 and also runs Union Glacier Camp.After the American
paraphrase: The Union Glacier Station, officially known as the Estación Polar Científica Conjunta Glaciar Unión (or Union Glacier Joint Scientific Polar Station), serves as Chile's seasonal Antarctic outpost. This facility is located on Union Glacier, within the Ellsworth Mountains. From November through January, the Chilean Antarctic Institute collaborates with Chile's three armed forces to manage its operations. The station officially opened its doors on January 4, 2014, with Chilean President Sebastián Piñera Echeñique presiding over the inauguration. Prior to this, in 2013, the Antarctic stations Teniente Arturo Parodi Alister and Antonio Huneeus were disassembled, and their respective equipment was moved to the Union Glacier Station. This move occurred subsequent to the November 2010 relocation of the operational activities of the American private enterprise, Antarctic Logistics & Expeditions LLC. This company has been responsible for operating the landing strip at Union Glacier since December 2008 and also oversees the Union Glacier Camp.

paraphrase: In El Salvador, the government's aggressive campaign against gangs, officially known as the Régimen de Excepción (State of Exception) and the Guerra Contra las Pandillas (War Against the Gangs), was initiated in March 2022. This offensive was a direct consequence of a surge in violence that occurred between March 25 and March 27, 2022, during which 87 individuals lost their lives across the nation. Attributing this drastic increase in homicides to the criminal organizations operating within the country, the Salvadoran government prompted the national legislature to endorse a state of emergency. This declaration led to the temporary suspension of fundamental rights, including the freedom of association and access to legal representation, and also extended the permissible period of detention without formal charges. These actions were part of a broader set of measures designed to amplify the authority of law enforcement agencies throughout El Salvador. By April 12, 2023, a total of 67,203 individuals, suspected of having connections to gangs, had been apprehended, contributing to significant overcrowding in the country's correctional facilities.

paraphrase: On February 6, 2023, the China Coast Guard and the Philippine Coast Guard experienced an incident close to the Second Thomas Shoal. These numerous demonstrations and protest initiatives have uniformly expressed dissatisfaction with the governing authorities in their particular nations. However, their scope has varied considerably, encompassing everything from demands for specific policy adjustments from the existing government to outright efforts aimed at dismantling the prevailing political system completely. original: Malaysia Airlines Flight 17 (MH17/MAS17) was a scheduled passenger flight from Amsterdam to Kuala Lumpur that was shot down by Russian-controlled forces on 17 July 2014, while flying over eastern Ukraine. All 283 passengers and 15 crew were killed. Contact with the aircraft, a Boeing 777-200ER, was lost when it was about 50 km (31 mi) from the Ukraine–Russia border, and wreckage from the aircraft fell near Hrabove in Donetsk Oblast, Ukraine, 40 km (25 mi) from the border. The shoot-down occurred during the war in Donbas over territory controlled by Russian separatist forces.The responsibility for investigation was delegated to the Dutch Safety Board (DSB) and the Dutch-led joint investigation team (JIT), who in 2016 reported that the airliner had been downed by a Buk surface-to-air missile launched
paraphrase: Malaysia Airlines Flight 17, also known as MH17 or MAS17, was a regularly scheduled passenger air service operating between Amsterdam and Kuala Lumpur. On July 17, 2014, this flight was brought down by Russian-controlled forces as it traversed eastern Ukraine. The incident resulted in the fatalities of all 283 passengers and 15 crew members aboard the aircraft. Communication with the Boeing 777-200ER ceased when it was approximately 50 kilometers (31 miles) away from the boundary between Ukraine and Russia. Debris from the aircraft subsequently landed near Hrabove in Ukraine's Donetsk Oblast, situated 40 kilometers (25 miles) from the border. This tragic event transpired amidst the conflict in Donbas, specifically over an area under the command of Russian separatist factions. The Dutch Safety Board (DSB) was entrusted with the primary investigative duties, alongside the Dutch-led joint investigation team (JIT). In 2016, their findings indicated that a Buk surface-to-air missile was responsible for bringing down the airliner.

original: The COVID-19 Response Recognition Award is a service award established by the New Zealand government in 2022 to recognise individuals and organisations who contributed to New Zealand’s frontline workforce COVID-19 response. The awards will be distributed from late January 2023, and it is expected that there will be about 80,000 individual recipients.Awards to individuals will take the form of a lapel pin and certificate, designed and manufactured by Mayer and Toye of Wellington. People eligible to receive the award include those who worked for at least a month in total as part of the managed isolation and quarantine (MIQ) system, at the border, in COVID-19 testing, vaccination or contact tracing, or as medical staff caring for COVID-19 patients.The organisational award, in the form of a digital certificate, will
paraphrase: The New Zealand government instituted the COVID-19 Response Recognition Award in 2022. This service award aims to acknowledge both individuals and organizations that played a part in New Zealand's frontline efforts in response to the COVID-19 pandemic. The distribution of these accolades is scheduled to commence in late January 2023, with an anticipated total of approximately 80,000 individual honorees. For individuals, the awards will consist of a lapel pin along with a certificate, both of which were conceived and produced by Mayer and Toye, located in Wellington. Individuals who qualify for this recognition include those who dedicated a cumulative minimum of one month of service within the managed isolation and quarantine (MIQ) framework, at the nation's borders, in COVID-19 testing operations, vaccination programs, or contact tracing initiatives, or as medical professionals attending to individuals afflicted with COVID-19. The award designated for organizations, presented as a digital certificate, will be bestowed upon them.

original: The 2014 Pentecost weekend storms in Europe were a series of intense supercells and MCSs affecting western and central Europe, which followed a heatwave in early June 2014, resulting from a Spanish plume synoptic weather pattern. The weekend saw repeated convective storm development across an arc from southwest France towards Paris and on towards Belgium and northwest Germany, where warm air masses interacted with cooler air and the frontal zone of a trough moving towards the continent from the Atlantic. Outbreaks of severe weather associated with this system spanned over 5 days from June 6 to June 11, with the worst damage occurring in the German state of North Rhine-Westphalia on 9 June, where the storm was described as one of the most violent in decades by the
paraphrase: A succession of powerful supercells and Mesoscale Convective Systems (MCSs) impacted western and central Europe during the Pentecost weekend of 2014. These meteorological events unfolded after a period of elevated temperatures in early June 2014, which were themselves a consequence of a synoptic weather phenomenon known as a Spanish plume. Throughout this particular weekend, recurrent instances of convective storm formation were observed along a curved path extending from the southwestern region of France, northward towards Paris, and then continuing into Belgium and the northwestern parts of Germany. In these areas, warm air masses encountered and interacted with cooler air, as well as with the frontal zone associated with a trough that was progressing eastward from the Atlantic Ocean towards the European continent. The severe weather episodes linked to this expansive system persisted for more than five days, specifically from June 6 through June 11. The most significant destruction and impact were recorded in the German state of North Rhine-Westphalia on June 9, where the storm was characterized by observers as one of the most ferocious to strike the region in many decades.

original: Local elections are being held in four municipalities in the north of Kosovo on 23 April 2023. They were initially scheduled to be held in December 2022 but were subsequently postoned. The elections are being held in the four Serb-majority municipalities of Leposavić, North Mitrovica, Zubin Potok and Zvečan, located in North Kosovo. The elections are being boycotted by the biggest Serb political party in Kosovo, the Serb List, following its departure from Kosovan institutions and the resignation of their mayors in North Kosovo in November. Among the declared candidates, there were only two from the Serb community. Aleksandar Arsenijević, candidate for mayor of the municipality of North Mitrovica withdrew his candidacy on 2 December. Only the Democratic Party of Kosovo (PDK) and Vetëvendosje (LVV) have mayoral candidates
paraphrase: In four northern municipalities of Kosovo, local elections are scheduled to take place on April 23, 2023. These elections were originally planned for December 2022 but were later postponed. The electoral process will occur in the four municipalities of Leposavić, North Mitrovica, Zubin Potok, and Zvečan, all situated in North Kosovo and predominantly inhabited by Serbs. The Serb List, which is the largest Serb political party in Kosovo, is boycotting these elections. This boycott follows their withdrawal from Kosovan institutions and the resignation of their mayors in North Kosovo in November. Out of all the individuals who announced their candidacy, only two were from the Serb community. On December 2, Aleksandar Arsenijević, who was a candidate for mayor in the municipality of North Mitrovica, withdrew his bid. Currently, only the Democratic Party of Kosovo (PDK) and Vetëvendosje (LVV) have put forward candidates for mayoral positions.
